markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has locked Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 console launch after two prior delays from the original Fall 2025 window, with the current date viewed as stable by insiders. This tight five-month runway from mid-June places heavy emphasis on the anticipated summer marketing push, expected to include Trailer 3, pre-order openings, and pricing details as early as late June. Ongoing GTA Online updates, including a potential final major content drop around mid-July, continue to sustain player engagement while Rockstar focuses resources on polishing the Vice City-set sequel. Industry-wide, the title's shadow has already shifted competing fall 2026 releases, amplifying trader focus on whether major hardware announcements, rival blockbuster launches, or other cultural milestones will precede it. Market sentiment reflects the high-stakes countdown, where confirmed marketing catalysts or any surprise updates could quickly shift implied probabilities around resolution criteria.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Weinstein’s advanced age, multiple health complications including cancer, and the May 2026 mistrial in his New York retrial have sharply tilted trader sentiment toward minimal additional incarceration. The California 16-year sentence remains under appeal while time already served and consecutive sentencing structures limit realistic outcomes, pushing the market-implied probability of no prison time to 82 percent. Ongoing appeals, potential further delays from his wheelchair-bound status at Rikers, and historical patterns of reduced effective sentences for elderly defendants reinforce the frontrunner positioning. Upcoming June hearings on whether prosecutors pursue another Mann retrial represent the next near-term catalyst that could shift these odds.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes hold a 3-2 series lead over the Vegas Golden Knights in the 2026 Stanley Cup Final, positioning them as the clear market leader at 79.5% implied probability. Carolina's dominant regular-season performance (53-22-7, 113 points) and deeper playoff run reflect superior depth, goaltending stability from Frederik Andersen, and consistent Eastern Conference dominance. Vegas (39-26-17, 95 points) reached the Final through resilience and timely scoring but faces elimination pressure after recent losses. Series swings have repeatedly flipped sportsbook favorites, yet traders continue to price the Hurricanes' overall edge and home-ice factors heavily in the remaining games.

markets.question: Will the San Antonio Spurs win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the San Antonio Spurs win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 81.5% implied probability in the 2026 NBA Champion market primarily because they lead the San Antonio Spurs 3-1 in the NBA Finals. New York erased a 29-point deficit for the largest comeback in Finals history during Game 4, shifting series momentum after splitting the first two contests and extending their advantage behind Jalen Brunson’s consistent scoring and Karl-Anthony Towns’ interior presence. The Spurs, who posted a strong regular-season record led by Victor Wembanyama, now face elimination and must overcome historical odds against rallying from a 3-1 deficit. Trader consensus reflects these confirmed results and roster health factors heading into Game 5.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: MegaETH airdrop by...?
events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit atop the World Cup winner market as the clear frontrunners, driven by their deep squads, recent major-tournament success (including Spain’s Euro 2024 title), and attacking depth featuring players like Lamine Yamal. Portugal, England, Brazil, and defending champion Argentina remain tightly bunched behind them, supported by star quality, strong qualifying campaigns, and favorable early draws. The expanded 48-team format and opening group-stage results have kept probabilities compressed, with minor shifts from injury news—such as Jurriën Timber’s absence for the Netherlands—yet the overall clustering underscores the competitive balance and high uncertainty across the field heading into the knockout rounds.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military through loyalist appointments and the lack of a designated successor, underpinning trader expectations that he will remain paramount leader past 2027. Term limits on the presidency were eliminated in 2018, enabling a precedent-breaking third term in 2022, with analyses pointing to a likely fourth term at the 2027 Party Congress. Recent diplomatic activity, including a May 2026 summit with U.S. President Trump and a planned June visit to North Korea, along with his New Year address emphasizing technological progress and policy continuity, underscores his active role without signs of internal challenges or health constraints. Potential successors remain unpromoted to the Politburo Standing Committee, reinforcing continuity over transition.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 23 percent amid a fragmented field where no candidate has formally entered the race. His position stems from high name recognition as California governor, early polling strength, and positioning on issues such as AI regulation that appeal across party factions. Other contenders including Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Jon Ossoff, and Kamala Harris trail due to lower visibility or narrower bases, while governors and senators like Josh Shapiro and Andy Beshear benefit from executive experience or regional appeal. Key differentiators include fundraising capacity, appeal to Black voters in early Southern contests, and ideological fit between moderate and progressive wings. Consolidation could accelerate after the 2026 midterms through endorsements, debate performances, or shifts in national polling that narrow the field.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 23 percent amid a fragmented field where no candidate has formally entered the race. His position stems from high name recognition as California governor, early polling strength, and positioning on issues such as AI regulation that appeal across party factions. Other contenders including Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Jon Ossoff, and Kamala Harris trail due to lower visibility or narrower bases, while governors and senators like Josh Shapiro and Andy Beshear benefit from executive experience or regional appeal. Key differentiators include fundraising capacity, appeal to Black voters in early Southern contests, and ideological fit between moderate and progressive wings. Consolidation could accelerate after the 2026 midterms through endorsements, debate performances, or shifts in national polling that narrow the field.
